canonical_definition: Q-Bench is characterized in the provided literature as a benchmark for multi-image evaluation. One paper defines its application for "in-domain multi-image evaluation" and lists it as one of two "existing benchmarks" adopted for its study. The primary documented use of Q-Bench is to operationalize and measure specific model capabilities. Across the surveyed work, it is reported to be a measure for both `Visual perception` and `Image description`. The abbreviation "QB" is also noted in one source as a shorthand for the benchmark.
papers: A-Bench: Are LMMs Masters at Evaluating AI-generated Images?
LLaVA-Interleave: Tackling Multi-image, Video, and 3D in Large Multimodal Models
TaskGalaxy: Scaling Multi-modal Instruction Fine-tuning with Tens of Thousands Vision Task Types
